The File class — reading and slicing netCDF files¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

class netcdf.File(object)¶

Public methods¶

File(filename,verbose = 0):
Parameters:
  • filename (string) – path to a hdf4, hdf5 or netcdf4 file or url to an opendap server
  • verbose (int) – sets the level of output during the operations

Create a File instance, opens the file and attempts to create objects for each of the dimensions.

registerDimension(name,officialName,class_instance,metadata_handler=None):
Parameters:
  • name (string) – name of the dimension (name of the variable in the netCDF file)
  • officialName (string) – name under which this dimension will be seen by the user
  • class_instance (class) – The class to instanciate when a dimension with the name name is encountered
  • metadata_handler – please see slicer.cf10.cf10metadata.CF10Metadata

This class method enables the registering of classes to instanciate when some particular dimensions are encountered, it acts as a factory.

dimensions():

returns list of instances of dimensions objects.

slice(varname,directive,visitor,**kargs):
Parameters:
  • varname (string) – name of the variable in the netCDF file.
  • directive (dictionary) – contains values to extract slices from the variable. The dictionary keys should correspond to offical names of the dimensions.
  • visitor – please see slicer.slicer.Slicer, same visitor
  • kargs – arguments to be passed to the Slicer and the visitor.

Call this method to slice a variable contained in the netCDF file.

readArray(directive,generator = None,**kargs):
Parameters:
  • directive (dictionary) – contains values to use to extract data including ‘variable’ defining the netCDF variable name.
  • generator (ArrayGenerator) – see slicing.arraygenerator.ArrayGenerator, don’t provide anything to obtain default behaviour

This method returns a numpy array with the shape and contents determined by the description in directive.

readFieldSet(self,directive,visitor == None,**kargs):
Parameters:
  • directive (dictionary) – contains values to use to extract data including ‘variable’ defining the netCDF variable name.
  • visitor – optional argument. The default visitor creates a fields.fieldset.FieldSet containing a collection of fields.field.Field. Please see netcdf.fieldcreator.FieldCreator

The default behaviour of this method using netcdf.fieldcreator.FieldCreator is to create a fieldset of fields corresponding to the selection given in directive.

Synonyms :readBundle

Private methods¶

createDimension(dimension,variable):

Instanciates dimension objects from the internal factory.

createVariableMap(file):
Parameters:file (netCDF4.Dataset) – enables to read the file

There are issues between opening files directly and accessing data through opendap, in one case variable names are in uppercase, in the other in lowercase. In order to make programs reusable, the File ignores case.

Warning :this causes another problem though, in some files (odas) variables exist in both uppercase and lowercase. In order to still be able to deal with these, lower case variables keep their name (e.g. variable) and upper case variable are called variable_upper.

This method returns a dictionary built according to the rules explained above.
